LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANSCHUTZ ENTERTAINMENT GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT TREATS DEFENDANT ANSCHUTZ ENTERTAINMENT GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION AS A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND GRANTS IT WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO ALLEGE THAT ANSCHUTZ ENTERTAINMENT GROUP, INC. IS THE ALTER EGO OF AEG CYCLING, LLC OR ANY OTHER RELEVANT ENTITY. THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO CONTINUE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY. ANSCHUTZ ENTERTAINMENT GROUP, INC. MAY RENEW ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTION AFTER DISCOVERY IS SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLETE IF IT DEEMS SUCH A FILING APPROPRIATE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

STATS DISPOSITION
Off Calendar Jan-27-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing on the Cross-Complaints of Cross-Complainants Anschutz Entertainment Group, Inc., a Colorado corporation, and AEG Cycling LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, as to the defaulted cross-defendant, Technical Works California, a California limited liability company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:50 a.m.

Richard D. Buckley, Esq. and Wendy Qiu, Esq. appeared for cross-complainants Anschutz Entertainment Group and AEG Cycling, LLC.
John Keenan, Officer, Assistant Secretary and General Counsel for cross-complainants, was placed under oath and testified.
Ms. Qiu, along with Messrs. Buckley and Keenan, appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by cross-defendant Technical Works California.

Plaintiff Flexible Funding LTD's underlying complaint was filed on October 7, 2019.
Plaintiff Flexible Funding LTD's First Amended Complaint was filed on March 20, 2021.
Dismissal without prejudice of plaintiff's First Amended Complaint was filed with the Clerk's Office on August 5, 2021.
Cross-complainant Anschultz Entertainment Group's cross-complaint against cross-defendant Technical Works California was filed on February 7, 2020.
Cross-complainant Anschultz Entertainment Group's first amended cross-complaint against cross-defendant Technical Works California was filed on March 12, 2020.
Substitute service of the Summons, First Amended Cross-Complaint and accompanying documents on cross-defendant Technical Works California occurred on March 13, 2020, and was effectuated on March 23, 2020.
Cross-complainant AEG Cycling, LLC's cross-complaint against cross-defendant Technical Works California was filed on May 20, 2020.
Substitute service of the Summons, Cross-Complaint and accompanying documents on cross-defendant Technical Works California occurred on June 16, 2020 and was effectuated on June 26, 2020.
Default as to cross-defendant Technical Works California on Anschutz Entertainment Group's First Amended Cross-Complaint was entered on April 21, 2020.
Default as to cross-defendant Technical Works California on AEG Cycling, LLC's cross-complaint was entered on July 27, 2020.
ROE cross-defendants were dismissed on June 5, 2020.
MOE cross-defendants were dismissed on August 17, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Cross-Complainants Anschutz Entertainment Group, Inc., a Colorado corporation, and AEG Cycling LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, and against the defaulted cross-defendant Technical Works California, a California limited liability company, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $190,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $9,160.80.
Attorney Fees - $398,111.50.
Costs - $9,636.77.

Total - $606,909.07.

Counsel for cross-complainants shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Proceedings concluded at 11:11 a.m.

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-15-2021 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Richard D. Buckley, Jr., Esq. of Arent Fox LLP, 213.443.7569, appearing for the Defendant/Moving Party Anschutz Entertainment Group, Inc.;
Atty. Ginger L. Sotelo, Esq., Pahl & McCay, 408.248.1000, appearing for Plaintiff Flexible Funding Ltd. Liability Co.

DEFENDANT ANSCHUTZ ENTERTAINMENT GROUP, INC.'s Motion for Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

Matter called, ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: The Court treats defendant Anschutz Entertainment Group, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment/adjudication as a motion for judgment on the pleadings and grants it with 20 days leave to amend. "[A] motion by a defendant under section 437c of the Code of Civil Procedure necessarily includes a test of the sufficiency of the complaint.... Motions for summary judgment in such situations have otherwise been allowed as being in legal effect motions for judgment on the pleadings." (C.L. Smith Co. v. Roger Ducharme, Inc. (1977) 65 Cal.App.3d 735, 745; see also Blanch v. Young (1984) 152 Cal.App.3d 1016, 1019; White v. County of Orange (1985) 166 Cal.App.3d 566 (disapproved on other grounds in John R. v. Oakland Unified School Dist. (1989) 48 Cal.3d 438).) Plaintiff has leave to allege that Anschutz Entertainment Group, Inc. is the alter ego of AEG Cycling, LLC or any other relevant entity. The parties are directed to continue to meet and confer regarding the outstanding discovery. Anschutz Entertainment Group, Inc. may renew its summary judgment/adjudication motion after discovery is substantially complete if it deems such a filing appropriate.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
